title: Key proximal-histidine bond to heme iron
prompt: In haemoglobin, show the key bond between the heme iron and the proximal histidine that holds the heme in place.
rubric: A heme (HEM) group is clearly visible
The proximal histidine (His F8) side chain is shown near the iron
An explicit bond/contact is drawn between the His NE2 and the heme iron
The rest of the protein is de-emphasised (cartoon or faded) so the contact is legible
notes: Graded by a VLM against the rubric, not by action match. reference_hint is one acceptable scene for calibration; His F8 is auth residue 87 on the alpha chain of 1HHO.
